# Code review iteration

Scope: repository-local code review of the Telegram fetcher implementation for bugs, maintainability risks, and refactoring targets. Specification-compliance gaps from the same pass were moved to `.github/reports/20260601T084613Z.md`. Findings already recorded in `.github/reviews/20260517T103458Z.md` are intentionally omitted.

Verification:
- `go test ./...` passes.
- `go vet ./...` fails on the logger mutex-copy issue below.

| Ref | Gap                                       | Severity |
| --- | ----------------------------------------- | -------- |
| R1  | [[#Slog handler copies a mutex by value]] | High     |

## Slog handler copies a mutex by value

Severity: **High**

`go vet ./...` reports `assignment copies lock value to clone` at `internal/log/log.go:117` and `internal/log/log.go:126`. `WithAttrs` and `WithGroup` copy the whole handler, including `sync.Mutex`, while the clones still write to the same `io.Writer`.

Impact: concurrent logging through grouped or attributed handlers can interleave output because each clone protects the shared writer with a different mutex. It also makes `go vet ./...` fail, so CI should reject this state.

Fix: move the writer and mutex into a shared pointer state and copy only that pointer when cloning handler metadata.
